// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor integrated with Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl control system, constrained within [-90, 90] degree operational range with safety cell enforcement for boundary protection."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingEnvironment
]

    base:desc "Safety cell component restricting operational boundaries of the robotic system to prevent unauthorized or hazardous movements."

]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLFunctionalArchitecture : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram representing the functional architecture of the MABI MAX 100 robot including components (motors, gearboxes) and their interfaces."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolingEnabler
]

instance SysMLLogicalArchitecture : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram representing the logical architecture of the MABI MAX 100 robot including component-connector relationships and data flows."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolingEnabler
]

instance SimulinkFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink executable model for friction analysis within the first axis of the robotic system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance SysMLStateMachine : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML v1.7 state machine model representing behavioral workflows for sensor data processing pipelines in the Digital Shadow."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolingEnabler
]

instance AggregatedDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured time-series dataset containing abstracted sensor data for manufacturing analytics including friction, motion accuracy, and sensor readings."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InputCurrentData, OutputAngleData, TorqueData, AngularVelocityData, AccelerationData, PositionDataAxis1, PositionDataAxis2, PositionDataAxis3, PositionDataAxis4, PositionDataAxis5, PositionDataAxis6
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance MotionAccuracyMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated metrics dataset representing motion accuracy deviations and performance indicators for the robotic system."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AggregatedDataTrace
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataVisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLToolingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML (Systems Modeling Language) framework enabling functional and logical architecture modeling of the physical system including Internal Block Diagrams and State Machines."
    DTDFVocab:enables SysMLFunctionalArchitectureService, SysMLLogicalArchitectureService, StateMachineBehavioralService
]

instance SimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink general-purpose simulation tool for detailed behavioral modeling including friction analysis simulations."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulinkFrictionAnalysisService
]

instance DataAggregationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom or third-party data aggregation layer processing raw sensor data into abstracted formats for manufacturing analytics."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService
]

instance DataVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Dashboard tools enabling real-time visualization of aggregated metrics including motion accuracy deviations and friction indicators."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

instance MQTTProtocolEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport) protocol enabling efficient, lightweight data transmission from physical sensors to the Digital Shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicalToVirtualDataFlowService
]

// C6: Services
instance SysMLFunctionalArchitectureService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing functional architecture modeling of the robotic system using SysML Internal Block Diagrams."
    DTDFVocab:provides FunctionalArchitectureInsight
]

instance SysMLLogicalArchitectureService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing logical architecture modeling of the robotic system using SysML Internal Block Diagrams."
    DTDFVocab:provides LogicalArchitectureInsight
]

]

instance SimulinkFrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing friction analysis simulations using MathWorks Simulink executable models."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionAnalysisInsight
]

instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service aggregating raw sensor data into structured, purpose-specific formats for manufacturing analytics."

]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling real-time visualization of aggregated metrics through interactive dashboards."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance PhysicalToVirtualDataFlowService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing automated one-way data flow from physical sensors to the Digital Shadow using MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataFlowInsight
]

instance FunctionalArchitectureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing the functional decomposition of the robotic system including component interfaces and interactions."
]

instance LogicalArchitectureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing the logical architecture of the robotic system including component-connector relationships."
]

]

    base:desc "Insight providing friction analysis results derived from Simulink executable models for the robotic system's joints."
]

    base:desc "Insight providing structured time-series datasets containing abstracted sensor data for manufacturing analytics."
]

    base:desc "Insight providing interactive dashboards displaying aggregated metrics including motion accuracy deviations and friction indicators."
]

]

// C1: Environment
instance ManufacturingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "High-precision manufacturing environment for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot with real-time motion control requirements."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, SafetyCell, PositionEncoderAxis1, PositionEncoderAxis2, PositionEncoderAxis3, PositionEncoderAxis4, PositionEncoderAxis5, PositionEncoderAxis6
]

// C5: Components
instance MotorComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical motor component of the MABI MAX 100 robot axis integrated with gearbox for precise kinematic control."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor
    base:isContainedIn AxisComponent
]

instance GearboxComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Mechanical gearbox component integrated with motor for torque amplification and speed reduction in the robotic system."
    base:isContainedIn AxisComponent
]

instance AxisComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Single axis component of the MABI MAX 100 robot including motor, gearbox, and position encoders for precise motion control."
    base:contains MotorComponent, GearboxComponent
    base:isContainedIn RoboticSystem
]

instance RoboticSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Complete MABI MAX 100 six-axis industrial robot system for manufacturing automation."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s temporal aspects are governed by the synchronization requirements between physical and digital components, which are implicitly addressed through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies. While explicit synchronization frequencies or latency requirements are not detailed in the provided documents, the integration of **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **SysML IBDs**, **AADL**, or **MontiArc**—combined with **behavioral modeling tools** like **MathWorks Simulink** or other simulation frameworks—implies the need for precise temporal alignment. The **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)** methodology further emphasizes layered abstraction, where physical-layer models (e.g., physics-based simulations) must align with higher-level logical and functional abstractions. Latency and granularity are implicitly managed through **state machines in SysML v1.7** or **MontiArc Automaton**, ensuring consistent temporal behavior across components. However, no specific time scales (e.g., real-time, near-real-time, or periodic updates) or synchronization protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, or DDS) are explicitly mentioned in the documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **orchestration and coordination** of the Digital Twin system is framed within **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** frameworks, leveraging **architecture patterns** from ADLs like **MontiArc** or **SysML** to define component interactions, interfaces, and connectors. Resource management is implicitly handled through **layered modeling** (e.g., requirements → functions → logic → physics), where each layer abstracts and refines system behavior. The **integration of simulation tools** (e.g., **Simulink**, other simulation methods) and **general-purpose languages** (e.g., C++, Java) suggests a **modular, service-oriented architecture** where components communicate via well-defined interfaces. However, no explicit **orchestration frameworks** (e.g., Kubernetes, service meshes) or **system-wide coordination mechanisms** (e.g., consensus protocols, federated learning) are mentioned. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Kritzinger et al.) differentiates passive data aggregation from active Digital Twin components, implying that the DT system requires **active components** (e.g., interpreters, processors) to enable bidirectional data flow, though the exact orchestration patterns remain unspecified."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. These stages are managed via methodologies like **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)**, **OOSEM**, **SPES**, or **SMArDT**, which systematically decompose system complexity. The lifecycle transitions between these stages involve **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which define **component-connector perspectives**, including interfaces, interconnections, and composition. Behavioral modeling is further refined using **state machines (SysML v1.7)** or **MontiArc Automaton**, often integrated with simulation tools like **MathWorks Simulink** or general-purpose languages (e.g., **C++/Java**). Lifecycle governance integrates **heterogeneous stakeholder perspectives** and ensures alignment across modeling notations and tools, emphasizing **interoperability** and **abstraction consistency** across phases. Transition management between stages adheres to **structured MBSE workflows**, ensuring traceability and version control through toolchain integration (e.g., **SysML + Simulink**)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is implicitly governed by the MBSE methodologies (e.g., RFLP, SPES) and the integration of SysML-based architecture models with simulation tools like Simulink, ensuring consistency across abstraction layers. Validation is inferred through the use of **position encoders with specified accuracy (0.1 mm)** and repeatability (0.06 mm), which serve as primary error quantification mechanisms. Uncertainty sources are not explicitly detailed, but the Digital Twin’s active components (e.g., control system integration) are designed to detect deviations via real-time feedback. Quality assurance processes rely on the structured MBSE framework and toolchain to mitigate model-simulation mismatches, though explicit error correction mechanisms are not described."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The document does not provide explicit details on the **technical network infrastructure** for the Digital Twin system. However, it references **communication flows** between physical and digital entities, implying the use of **streaming protocols** (e.g., MQTT for sensor data transmission). No specifics on communication protocols (e.g., OPC UA, Modbus TCP), network topology (e.g., star, mesh), bandwidth/latency requirements, redundancy mechanisms, or security protocols (e.g., TLS, VPN) are mentioned beyond the general context of **heterogeneous data integration** and **automated one-way data flow**."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The document describes the Digital Twin system’s deployment context indirectly through references to **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies and tools like **SysML**, **Simulink**, and **general-purpose languages (C++, Java)** for behavior modeling. It mentions the need to integrate **heterogeneous models and tools** (e.g., simulation environments, architecture description languages) but does not specify hosting platforms (cloud/edge/on-premise), computational/storage requirements, scalability mechanisms, or deployment models. The only concrete deployment-related detail is the use of **local simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink) for behavior description, implying on-premise or hybrid deployment for simulation purposes. No details on orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes), availability/reliability assurance, or dashboard deployment are provided."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration is addressed through **heterogeneous system modeling** and **interoperability standards** inherent to MBSE methodologies. The document highlights the need to integrate **multiple modeling notations** (e.g., SysML, ADLs) and **tools** (e.g., Simulink, general-purpose languages) to describe system structure and behavior. While no specific **data exchange protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, REST, SOAP) or **interoperability standards** (e.g., ISO/IEC 42010, IEEE 1471) are cited, the **component & connector perspective** of ADLs like **ACME** or **AADL** implies support for standardized interfaces. Dependencies on **external systems** (e.g., enterprise systems, IoT platforms) are not detailed, but the mention of **heterogeneous stakeholders** suggests integration patterns may align with **enterprise architecture frameworks** (e.g., TOGAF, Zachman). No explicit mention is made of **Digital Twin-to-Digital Twin integration** or **cross-domain interoperability** mechanisms."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The provided documents reference a **physical safety cell** (as per [44] MABI robotic arm specifications) to restrict the operational workspace of the robotic system, ensuring operator safety by confining movement to predefined boundaries. Additionally, [42] describes the use of **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)** for data streaming between digital twin components, which inherently supports lightweight, publish-subscribe communication for real-time monitoring and control. However, no explicit mention of cybersecurity protocols (e.g., TLS, OPC UA security), access control mechanisms, or safety-critical standards (e.g., IEC 61508, ISO 13849) is found in the documents. The context does not detail authentication systems, threat mitigation strategies, or fail-safe mechanisms beyond the physical safety cell."
]
